---
title: "Executive Summary — Cell-Type-Specific Biomarkers in Human Liver Fibrosis"
date: today
params:
  results_dir: "results"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
# | tags: [parameters]
results_dir = "results"
```

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd

RD = Path(results_dir)
scores = (
    pd.read_csv(RD / "08_score/candidate_scores.tsv", sep="\t")
    if (RD / "08_score/candidate_scores.tsv").exists()
    else pd.DataFrame()
)
```

## Objective

Discover and prioritize **cell-type-specific biomarkers and therapeutic targets** in human
liver fibrosis from single-cell transcriptomics (primary: GSE136103, cirrhotic vs healthy;
validation: GSE244832, MASLD/MASH F0–F4), focusing on the hepatic stellate/myofibroblast,
macrophage/monocyte, and endothelial compartments.

## Approach (one paragraph)

A reproducible Snakemake + Scanpy pipeline ingests and QCs the data, integrates over donor
(not disease) and verifies fibrosis signal is preserved, annotates and validates the three
disease compartments, runs **donor-aware pseudobulk** differential expression, characterizes
mechanism via pathway/TF activity and ligand–receptor analysis (with downstream-target
corroboration), and prioritizes candidates with a transparent composite score combining DE
effect, cell-type specificity, cross-dataset reproducibility, druggability, biomarker
accessibility, and an explainable ML (XGBoost + SHAP) signal.

## Top candidates

```{python}
if not scores.empty:
    cols = [c for c in ["rank", "gene", "compartment", "composite", "direction"] if c in scores.columns]
    display(scores.sort_values("rank").head(20)[cols] if cols else scores.head(20))
```

## Translational interpretation

The ranked table flags, per compartment, which candidates are most relevant for **diagnostics**
(secreted/accessible, reproducible across datasets) versus **therapeutic targeting** (druggable,
mechanistically central in the fibrotic crosstalk). See the full report and
`results/08_score/candidate_scores.tsv` for the per-candidate rationale.
